title: Pancrase

infobox.value: [Pro Wrestling Fujiwara Gumi](/wiki/Pro_Wrestling_Fujiwara_Gumi "Pro Wrestling Fujiwara Gumi") World Pancrase Create Inc.
* [Masakatsu Funaki](/wiki/Masakatsu_Funaki "Masakatsu Funaki") * [Minoru Suzuki](/wiki/Minoru_Suzuki "Minoru Suzuki") [Ken Shamrock](/wiki/Ken_Shamrock "Ken Shamrock")

(パンクラス, *Pankurasu*) is a Japanese [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") (MMA) [promotion company](/wiki/Promoter_(entertainment) "Promoter (entertainment)") based in [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"). It was founded in 1993 by professional wrestlers [Masakatsu Funaki](/wiki/Masakatsu_Funaki "Masakatsu Funaki") and [Minoru Suzuki](/wiki/Minoru_Suzuki "Minoru Suzuki").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

MMA promotion based in Japan

Pancrase Inc.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Company type | Private |
| Industry | [Mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") [promotion](/wiki/Promoter_(entertainment) "Promoter (entertainment)") |
| Predecessor | [Pro Wrestling Fujiwara Gumi](/wiki/Pro_Wrestling_Fujiwara_Gumi "Pro Wrestling Fujiwara Gumi") World Pancrase Create Inc. |
| Founded | 1993; 33 years ago (1993) |
| Founders | * [Masakatsu Funaki](/wiki/Masakatsu_Funaki "Masakatsu Funaki") * [Minoru Suzuki](/wiki/Minoru_Suzuki "Minoru Suzuki") [Ken Shamrock](/wiki/Ken_Shamrock "Ken Shamrock") |
| Headquarters | [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo") , [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan") |
| Key people | Hidetaka Fukui (chief chairman) |
| Owner | * Pancrase Executive Committee |
| Website | [pancrase.co.jp](https://pancrase.co.jp) |

**Pancrase** (パンクラス, *Pankurasu*) is a Japanese [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") (MMA) [promotion company](/wiki/Promoter_(entertainment) "Promoter (entertainment)") based in [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"). It was founded in 1993 by professional wrestlers [Masakatsu Funaki](/wiki/Masakatsu_Funaki "Masakatsu Funaki") and [Minoru Suzuki](/wiki/Minoru_Suzuki "Minoru Suzuki").

The name was based on [pankration](/wiki/Pankration "Pankration"), a fighting sport in the Ancient Olympic Games. Suzuki and Funaki practiced [catch wrestling](/wiki/Catch_wrestling "Catch wrestling"). They based the promotion and its rules on professional wrestling. The promotion's champions were called "King of Pancrase".

The rules allowed closed-fisted punches, except to the head, and palm strikes to the head. A wrestler must break a submission hold when the opponent reaches the ropes, but a wrestler who claims a rope break loses a point. A wrestler who claims a specified number of rope breaks (between 3 and 5) was disqualified. From 1998 to 2000, the promotion changed its rules to resemble other mixed martial arts promotions.

Beginning in 2007, Pancrase: Legends of Mixed Martial Arts aired weekly on ImaginAsian TV. The network folded in 2011.

In 2008, the Fight Network began airing Pancrase events in Canada and the United Kingdom.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Establishment and first event

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Establishment and first event")]

In 1993, [Masakatsu Funaki](/wiki/Masakatsu_Funaki "Masakatsu Funaki"), [Minoru Suzuki](/wiki/Minoru_Suzuki "Minoru Suzuki"), [Ken Shamrock](/wiki/Ken_Shamrock "Ken Shamrock"), [Takaku Fuke](/wiki/Takaku_Fuke "Takaku Fuke"), and others from the shoot-style [Fujiwara Gumi](/wiki/Fujiwara_Gumi "Fujiwara Gumi") wrestling promotion left the company, due to (mentor and promoter) [Yoshiaki Fujiwara](/wiki/Yoshiaki_Fujiwara "Yoshiaki Fujiwara")'s decision to cooperate with the likes of [Universal Lucha Libre](/wiki/Universal_Lucha_Libre "Universal Lucha Libre") and W\*ING and favoring showmanship over in-ring work. As suggested by [Karl Gotch](/wiki/Karl_Gotch "Karl Gotch"), Pancrase was formed by Funaki, Suzuki and Fuke, and the promotion would focus on pure shoot-style [wrestling](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling") with limited gimmicks and no predetermined outcomes.

Funaki and Suzuki were students of wrestler [Antonio Inoki](/wiki/Antonio_Inoki "Antonio Inoki"). They were inspired by the 1976 [Muhammad Ali vs. Antonio Inoki](/wiki/Muhammad_Ali_vs._Antonio_Inoki "Muhammad Ali vs. Antonio Inoki") event that pit a boxer against a wrestler.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On May 16, 1993, they established **World Pancrase Create Inc.** (WPC) with the capital of 30,000,000 Japanese yen.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

On September 21, 1993, Pancrase promoted their first event at [Tokyo Bay NK Hall](/wiki/Tokyo_Bay_NK_Hall "Tokyo Bay NK Hall"). All the matches were finished with knockout and submission.

### Early era

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early era")]

The first King Of Pancrase Open Weight champion was [Ken Shamrock](/wiki/Ken_Shamrock "Ken Shamrock") (later becoming famous for his exploits in the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")), who had previous experience in the newborn [UWF](/wiki/Universal_Wrestling_Federation_(Japan) "Universal Wrestling Federation (Japan)") and Fujiwara Gumi. Over the years, Funaki and Suzuki held the title alongside other big names, such as [Bas Rutten](/wiki/Bas_Rutten "Bas Rutten"), [Frank Shamrock](/wiki/Frank_Shamrock "Frank Shamrock") and [Guy Mezger](/wiki/Guy_Mezger "Guy Mezger").

Pancrase now recognizes King of Pancrase titles in the super heavyweight, heavyweight, light heavyweight, middleweight and welterweight divisions, and there are plans to award new lightweight and featherweight champions in the future. The open weight title was recently restored and is currently held by [Josh Barnett](/wiki/Josh_Barnett "Josh Barnett"), who formerly competed in [PRIDE](/wiki/Pride_Fighting_Championships "Pride Fighting Championships") and the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"). Minoru Suzuki no longer competes for Pancrase, as he returned to regular professional wrestling in 2003 as a freelancer.

### Founding of Pancrase Korea

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Founding of Pancrase Korea")]

Pancrase Korea is the [South Korean](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea") subsidiary of the Pancrase Inc. and held several events from 2007 to 2010. The first Pancrase Korea event was an amateur one held on March 10, 2007, in [Busan](/wiki/Busan "Busan") and the last one was the semi-professional *Neo-Blood Tournament 2 & Hybrid Challenge 12* held on September 12, 2010, in [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Changes in management

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Changes in management")]

In May 2008, "**Pancrase Inc.**" was established as a new management company for Pancrase, and WPC alienated Pancrase Inc. their all of business as on May 27, 2008. When Pancrase Inc. was established, Yasushi Sakamoto, a managing director of WPC, was installed as the executive president.

On October 14, 2008, Pancrase Inc. moved its office to [Nishi Ikebukuro in the ward of Toshima](/wiki/Toshima,_Tokyo "Toshima, Tokyo") in [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In September 2009, Pancrase Inc. moved its office from [Nishi Ikebukuro](/wiki/Toshima,_Tokyo "Toshima, Tokyo") to [Aobadai in ward of Meguro](/wiki/Aobadai,_Meguro,_Tokyo "Aobadai, Meguro, Tokyo") in Tokyo, and moved again to [Kameido in the ward of Koto](/wiki/Koto,_Tokyo "Koto, Tokyo").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

On October 1, 2010, [Ryo Kawamura](/wiki/Ryo_Kawamura "Ryo Kawamura"), who was the current [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") was installed as the executive president. Yasushi Sakamoto, the former president, had become a managing director.

Kawamura held a press conference in [Yokohama, Kanagawa](/wiki/Yokohama,_Kanagawa "Yokohama, Kanagawa"), on October 22, and explained about restructuring and announced that "**Commission Council**" had been established as a third party
.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

On June 1, 2012, Pancrase announced that the promotion had been sold to Masakazu Sakai and his [Smash](/wiki/Smash_(professional_wrestling) "Smash (professional wrestling)") organization, with Sakai taking over as the new president and his partner [Yuji Shimada](/wiki/Yuji_Shimada "Yuji Shimada") as the event producer of the promotion. As part of the reconstruction of Pancrase, the new owners intend to replace rings with cages, holding events to test the new format in 2013.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Pancrase 257 on March 30, 2014, was the last event where Pancrase used a ring, and by Pancrase 258 on May 11, 2014, Pancrase adopted the decagon or the ten-sided cage of their American partners [World Series of Fighting](/wiki/World_Series_of_Fighting "World Series of Fighting").[[14]](#cite_note-:0-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Organizational change

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Organizational change")]

On March 9, 2022, the Smash Martial Arts Department Pancrase Business Headquarters will be reorganized and operated as the "Pancrase Executive Committee".

In addition, the King of Pancrase Executive Committee will also be reorganized and operated as the "King of Pancrase Council".[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Rules

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Rules")]

### Original rules

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Original rules")]

Aside from the traditional grappling rules (e.g., no biting, no eye-gouging) the rules were formerly as follows:

* No elbows to the head (neither while standing nor on the ground).
* No closed-fist strikes to the head (neither while standing nor on the ground).
* No knees to the head on the ground.
* No kicks/stomps to the head on the ground.
* If a participant gets too close to the ropes, he is stood back up on the feet. (as opposed to PRIDE's re-centering in the middle of the ring)
* Non-title matches consist of one 15 minute round, while title matches consist of one 30 minute round.
* If a participant is caught in a submission and taps out (whether unable to reach the ropes or not), it is a loss.
* Five "escapes" are given to each fighter at the start of every match. An escape can be used when caught in a submission near the ropes, in which case the participant can grab them, be stood back on the feet and have one point deducted. Once a participant has used all of his escapes, it is a loss.
* For knockouts, a ten-count similar to boxing and kickboxing is used. If the participant is unable to answer the ten-count, it is declared a TKO and that fighter has lost the match. However, if the participant is able to answer the ten-count, the fight resumes and one point is deducted.
* If, at the end of regulated time, neither fighter has submitted, been knocked out, or lost all their points, a decision is rendered based on who lost fewer points. If neither fighter lost any points, or both lost the same number of points, the fight is declared a draw.
* In the 1994 King of Pancrase tournament, the rules were slightly different, in that the first round had one 10-minute round and three rope escapes, and the finals had one 20-minute round with three rope escapes.
* For a short period of time when [Bas Rutten](/wiki/Bas_Rutten "Bas Rutten") was the King of Pancrase, heelhooks were banned because of the frequency of injuries.
* Strikes on the ground – whether closed fist to the body or open hand to the head – were discouraged by the fans, but still legal.

### Present-day Japanese rules

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Present-day Japanese rules")]

After [Pancrase: Breakthrough 1](/wiki/1999_in_Pancrase#Pancrase:_Breakthrough_1 "1999 in Pancrase") held on January 19, 1999, Pancrase began to use a ruleset similar to that of [Pride FC](/wiki/Pride_FC "Pride FC"), but prohibits knees to the head of grounded opponents.

In May 2016, Pancrase adopted the [Association of Boxing Commissions and Combative Sports](/wiki/Association_of_Boxing_Commissions "Association of Boxing Commissions") rules, better known as the [Unified Rules of Mixed Martial Arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts_rules "Mixed martial arts rules").

## Current Pancrase champions (King and Queen of Pancrase)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pancrase&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Current Pancrase champions (King and Queen of Pancrase)")]

Main article: [List of Pancrase champions](/wiki/List_of_Pancrase_champions "List of Pancrase champions")

The current weight division system of Pancrase is based upon the [Unified Rules of Mixed Martial Arts](/wiki/Unified_Rules_of_Mixed_Martial_Arts "Unified Rules of Mixed Martial Arts") since December 4, 2007. At this time, Pancrase also announced establishment of [Flyweight](/wiki/Flyweight_(MMA) "Flyweight (MMA)") and [Bantamweight](/wiki/Bantamweight_(MMA) "Bantamweight (MMA)"), and abolition of [Super heavyweight](/wiki/Super_heavyweight_(MMA) "Super heavyweight (MMA)"). On March 18, 2011, Pancrase restructured their weight classes adding several more lower weight classes. In addition, they introduced the Queen Of Pancrase title which will be used for women's fights in the future.[[17]](#cite_note-19)

### Former Pancrase champions
